intended_use: The SIGNA Premier system is a whole body magnetic resonance scanner designed to support high resolution, high signal-to-noise ratio, and short scan times. It is indicated for use as a diagnostic imagi
intended_use_full: The SIGNA Premier system is a whole body magnetic resonance scanner designed to support high resolution, high signal-to-noise ratio, and short scan times. It is indicated for use as a diagnostic imaging device to produce axial, sagittal, coronal, and oblique images, spectroscopic images, parametric maps, and/or spectra, dynamic images of the structures and/or functions of the entire body, including, but not limited to, head, neck, TMJ, spine, breast, heart, abdomen, pelvis, joints, prostate, blood vessels, and musculoskeletal regions of the body.
other_metrics: Verification and validation testing completed with passing results per predefined pass/fail criteria; internal scans confirmed productivity and consistency benefits

safety_events.narrative_text: THE INVESTIGATION BY GE HEALTHCARE (GEHC) HAS BEEN COMPLETED. TESTING CONCLUDED THAT ALL SAFETY MITIGATING DEVICES WERE FUNCTIONAL, AND THE SYSTEM MET ALL SPECIFICATIONS AS DESIGNED WITH ONE EXCEPTION OF COHERENT NOISE. COHERENT NOISE DOES NOT IMPACT THE SAFETY OF THE SYSTEM NOR THE COIL PERFORMANCE. THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE INJURY WAS DETERMINED TO BE INADEQUATE PATIENT PADDING FOR THE MRI PROCEDURE. THE OPERATOR MANUAL AND GENERAL CLINICAL PRACTICE RECOMMENDED THAT ADEQUATE PADDING BETWEEN THE PATIENT AND THE COIL /CABLE IS REQUIRED TO PREVENT ANY ANATOMICAL LOOPING THAT COULD RESULT IN WARMING OR BURNS. THE OPERATOR DID NOT HAVE ADEQUATE PADDING NOR USE THE PROPER COIL CABLE ROUTING TO PREVENT ANY POSSIBLE BURNS. THE OPERATOR DOCUMENTATION DESCRIBES THE APPROPRIATE SAFETY MEASURES FOR PADDING PATIENTS FOR MR EXAMS. THE MR OPERATOR HAS THE FINAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE USE AND PLACEMENT OF NON-CONDUCTIVE MR COMPATIBLE PADDING AND PREPARATION OF THE PATIENT, PRIOR TO STARTING THE MR EXAM PROCEDURE. NO FURTHER ACTIONS ARE PLANNED BY GEHC.
